text: Walking to my car late at night, I kept looking over my shoulder and gripping my keys tightly. My body felt ready to run.
Before opening the message, I felt a jolt of dread, as if bad news was guaranteed. My stomach dropped immediately.
I am avoiding decisions because I keep imagining the worst possible outcome. The uncertainty feels threatening, not neutral.
My chest tightens whenever I think about what might happen next. I feel unsafe even in ordinary situations.
I keep scanning the room and listening for danger signs. My mind is stuck in high alert mode.
I feel intimidated by the risk and cannot stop preparing for catastrophe. It is hard to trust that things will be okay.
When the plan changed suddenly, I froze and could not respond clearly. Fear took over before logic could catch up.
I am afraid to take the next step because I expect something harmful to happen. Every option feels dangerous.
My thoughts keep jumping to threat scenarios, and I feel physically tense all day. Relaxing feels impossible.
I feel vulnerable and exposed, like I have no real protection in this situation.
Even small noises startle me right now, and my body reacts before I can think. The fear feels immediate and real.
I am hesitant to commit because I expect loss or harm if I choose wrong. Caution has turned into fear.
I keep postponing action because my fear response keeps telling me to retreat.
The uncertainty is not exciting; it feels like a threat I cannot control.
I feel fear in my body, in my thoughts, and in every decision I try to make.
